# terrible_community_general_pubnub_blocks (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `application` (String) Name of target PubNub application for which blocks configuration on specific `keyset` is done.
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `keyset` (String) Name of application's keys set which is bound to managed blocks.
- `name` (String) Name of managed block which is later visible on admin.pubnub.com.

### Optional

- `account` (String) Name of PubNub account for from which `application` is used to manage blocks. User's account is used if value not set or empty.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `cache` (String) In case if single play use blocks management module few times it is preferred to enabled 'caching' by making previous module to share gathered artifacts and pass them to this parameter.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `changes` (String) List of fields which should be changed by block itself (does not affect any event handlers). Possible options for change is: `name`.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) Short block description which is later visible on https://admin.pubnub.com. Used only if block does not exists and does not change description for existing block.
- `email` (String) Email from account for which new session should be started. Not required if `cache` contains result of previous module call (in same play).
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `event_handlers` (String) List of event handlers which should be updated for specified block `name`. Each entry for new event handler should contain: `name`, `src`, `channels`, `event`. `name` used as event handler name which can be used later to make changes to it. `src` is full path to file with event handler code. `channels` is name of channel from which event handler is waiting for events. `event` is type of event which is able to trigger event handler: `js-before-publish`, `js-after-publish`, `js-after-presence`. Each entry for existing handlers should contain `name` (so target handler can be identified). Rest parameters (`src`, `channels` and `event`) can be added if changes required for them. It is possible to rename event handler by adding `changes` key to event handler payload and pass dictionary, which contains single key `name`, where new name should be passed. To remove particular event handler it is possible to set `state` for it to `absent` and it is removed.
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `password` (String) Password which match to account to which specified `email` belong. Not required if `cache` contains result of previous module call (in same play).
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Intended block state after event handlers creation / update process is completed.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `validate_certs` (Boolean) This key allow to try skip certificates check when performing REST API calls. Sometimes host may have issues with certificates on it and this causes problems to call PubNub REST API. If check should be ignored `false` should be passed to this parameter.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `module_cache` (String) Cached account information. In case if with single play module used few times it is better to pass cached data to next module calls to speed up process.
